title: MyCrochetKit Emergency Fixes
description: Critical fixes to restore site functionality

userStories.title: Fix Firebase API Key Error
userStories.description: The live site shows 'auth/invalid-api-key' error. Firebase keys are hardcoded but deployment is failing.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub Actions deployment succeeds without errors
Live site at mycrochetkit.com loads without Firebase errors
Console shows '🔥 Firebase Config: Initializing main services (v1.0.1)' log
Users can sign in and use the app
technicalNotes: GitHub Actions failing at 'Install Dependencies' step
Need to fix package-lock.json or add --legacy-peer-deps
Firebase keys are already hardcoded in src/lib/firebase.ts and src/config/firebase.ts
Alternative: manual deploy with 'npx firebase-tools@13 deploy --only hosting:production --project my-crochetkit'

userStories.title: Fix GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
userStories.description: The 'Install Dependencies' step in GitHub Actions is failing, preventing automatic deployments.
acceptanceCriteria: All steps (Install, Build, Deploy) pass with green checkmarks
Pushing to main branch triggers successful deployment
technicalNotes: Check if --legacy-peer-deps is needed in npm ci command
Verify package-lock.json is in sync with package.json
Check for any missing or incompatible dependencies

userStories.title: Add environment variable security
userStories.description: Move hardcoded Firebase keys back to environment variables once deployment is fixed.
acceptanceCriteria: Firebase keys loaded from environment variables
Fallback to hardcoded values if env vars missing (for local dev)
No sensitive keys exposed in source code
technicalNotes: Add FIREBASE_* secrets to GitHub repository settings
Update deploy.yml to use secrets instead of hardcoded values
Keep fallback values for local development
